name: CMB blackbody spectrum (FIRAS)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the near-perfect blackbody spectrum of the CMB measured by COBE-FIRAS: T_CMB = 2.7255 +/- 0.0006 K with deviations from a Planck spectrum (mu and y distortions) bounded at |mu| < 9 x 10^-5 and |y| < 1.5 x 10^-5.

regime.description: Microwave wavelengths, photon thermalization era at z > 2 x 10^6 (mu epoch) and 2 x 10^4 < z < 2 x 10^6 (y epoch).

current_bound.reference: Fixsen et al., 'The cosmic microwave background spectrum from the full COBE FIRAS data set', ApJ 473, 576 (1996); Mather et al., ApJ 420, 439 (1994)

what_it_excludes: Constrains any energy-injection process between z ~ 2 x 10^6 and recombination: decaying or annihilating particles, primordial black hole evaporation, dissipation of acoustic modes. Severely limits scenarios with non-thermal photon production at MeV-eV scales between BBN and recombination.

parameterization.name: Spectral distortion parameters mu (chemical-potential distortion from energy injection in mu-era) and y (Compton-y distortion from energy injection in y-era).
parameterization.reference: Chluba and Sunyaev, MNRAS 419, 1294 (2012); Hu and Silk, PRD 48, 485 (1993).
parameterization.summary: Energy injection per unit time Q_dot maps to Delta mu, y via well-known formulas; observables computed by integrating over redshift weighted by the appropriate visibility functions.

procedural.predicted_observable: CMB spectrum measured against a Planck blackbody at T_CMB; deviations parameterized as mu and y plus residual relative-intensity distortion.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Upper bounds as quoted; PIXIE / PRISTINE proposals aim to improve by 3 orders of magnitude.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts any pre-recombination energy injection, compute its mu and y signatures.
Include photon-baryon thermalization timescales (Compton, double-Compton, bremsstrahlung).

primary_references.citation: Fixsen et al., 'The cosmic microwave background spectrum from the full COBE FIRAS data set', ApJ 473, 576 (1996)
Chluba and Sunyaev, 'The evolution of CMB spectral distortions in the early universe', MNRAS 419, 1294 (2012)
